name_romaji: Ryūseikaku Kyūzō Takehisa Yumeji Korekushon
name_en: Takehisa Yumeji collection formerly held by Ryūseikaku
description_en: An art collection, classified as a tangible cultural property (painting and calligraphy). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2022. It is located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Kenpon Bokusho Kakejiku 'Hie Jinja' Yamaoka Tesshū Hitsu
name_en: Hanging scroll with the calligraphy 'Hie Jinja' by Yamaoka Tesshū, ink on silk
description_en: A calligraphic hanging scroll attributed by inscription to Yamaoka Tesshū, classified as a tangible cultural property (painting and calligraphy). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2024. It is located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Edojō Ato Shutsudo Honmaru Kankei Shiryō
name_en: Materials related to the Honmaru excavated from the site of Edo Castle
description_en: A collection of excavated materials, classified as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2014. They are located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Kioichō Iseki Shutsudo Kishū Hantei Kankei Shiryō
name_en: Materials related to the Kishū Domain residence excavated from the Kioichō Site
description_en: A collection of excavated materials, classified as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2000. They are located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Seiganin Ato Shutsudo Shiryō
name_en: Materials excavated from the site of Seigan-in Temple
description_en: A collection of excavated materials, classified as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2013. They are located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Tōkyō Eki Yaesu Kitaguchi Iseki Shutsudo Kirishitan Kankei Shiryō
name_en: Christian-related materials excavated from the Tokyo Station Yaesu North Exit Site
description_en: A collection of excavated materials, classified as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2006. They are located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Hirakawachō Iseki Shutsudo Shiryō
name_en: Materials excavated from the Hirakawachō Site
description_en: A collection of excavated materials, classified as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1991. They are located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Yūrakuchō Itchōme Iseki Shutsudo Matsudaira Fujiike Kankei Shiryō
name_en: Materials related to the Matsudaira Fujii family excavated from the Yūrakuchō 1-chōme Site
description_en: A collection of excavated materials, classified as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2016. They are located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Tamagawa Jōsui no Ishimasu, Mokuhi (Kōjimachi Sanchōme Nibanchi Saki Shutsudo)
name_en: Stone junction box and wooden conduit of the Tamagawa Aqueduct (excavated near Kōjimachi 3-chōme 2-banchi)
description_en: Excavated waterworks components, classified as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2018. The stone junction box is in Kiyosumidani Park at Kioichō 2, and the wooden conduit is at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Edo Tegaki Chōchin Mojiire
name_en: Hand-painted lettering of Edo paper lanterns
description_en: A craft technique, classified as an intangible cultural property (craft technique). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2023. The associated municipality is Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Shimotsuke Minagawa Han Matsudairake Bosho
name_en: Gravesite of the Matsudaira family, lords of the Minagawa Domain in Shimotsuke Province
description_en: A family gravesite, classified as a historic site in the monuments category. It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2007. It is located in the precincts of Shinpō-ji Temple at Kōjimachi 6-4-2, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Kyū Iike Nakayashiki no Inumaki to Kaya
name_en: Inumaki and kaya trees of the former Ii family middle residence
description_en: An inumaki (Podocarpus) tree and a kaya (Japanese nutmeg-yew) tree, classified as natural monuments in the monuments category. They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2009. They are located on the grounds of the New Otani at Kioichō 4-1, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Endōke Kyū Tenpo, Jūtaku Omoya
name_en: Former shop and main residential building of the Endō family
description_en: A building, classified as a tangible cultural property (architecture). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2008. It is located in Miyamoto Park at Sotokanda 2-19, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Kōjimuro tsuketari Meiji Sanjūnananen Kōjimuro no Zu
name_en: Kōji fermentation chamber, with a drawing of the chamber from 1904 (Meiji 37)
description_en: A kōji fermentation chamber with an accompanying drawing, classified as a tangible cultural property (architecture). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2009. The chamber is at Amanoya, Sotokanda 2-18-15, and the drawing is at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Hie Jinja Keidai Massha Sannō Inari Jinja Honden
name_en: Main sanctuary of Sannō Inari Shrine, a subsidiary shrine in the precincts of Hie Shrine
description_en: A shrine building, classified as a tangible cultural property (architecture). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1988. It is located at Hie Shrine, Nagatachō 2-10-5, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Hirakawa Tenmangū Dō Torii
name_en: Bronze torii gate of Hirakawa Tenmangū Shrine
description_en: A bronze torii gate, classified as a tangible cultural property (architecture). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1994. It is located in the precincts of Hirakawa Tenmangū Shrine at Hirakawachō 1-7-5, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Kaei Yon Idoshi Kugatsu Kanda Myōjin Sairei Goyōdome
name_en: Official record of the Kanda Myōjin festival, ninth month of Kaei 4 (year of the boar)
description_en: A historical record book, classified as a tangible cultural property (old documents). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2008. It is located at Kanda Shrine, Sotokanda 2-16-2, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Kanda Kamezumichō Monjo
name_en: Documents of Kanda Kamezumichō
description_en: A collection of documents, classified as a tangible cultural property (old documents). They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2010. They are located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Kyū Kanda-ku Fukkō Shōgakkō Kenchiku Kankei Monjo
name_en: Documents relating to the construction of reconstruction elementary schools in the former Kanda Ward
description_en: A collection of documents, classified as a tangible cultural property (old documents). They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2017. They are located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Gojōraku Goyōdome
name_en: Official record concerning the shogunal journey to Kyoto
description_en: A historical record book, classified as a tangible cultural property (old documents). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2010. It is located at Kanda Shrine, Sotokanda 2-16-2, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Hara Taneaki Kyūzō Shiryō
name_en: Materials formerly in the collection of Hara Taneaki
description_en: A collection of documents and materials, classified as a tangible cultural property (old documents). They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2009. They are located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Hie Jinja Shozō Shamusho Nisshi (Kan'ei Rokunen kara Meiji Jūnen)
name_en: Shrine office diaries held by Hie Shrine (Kan'ei 6 to Meiji 10)
description_en: A set of diaries, classified as a tangible cultural property (old documents). They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1991. They are located at Hie Shrine, Nagatachō 2-10-5, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Hie Jinja Shozō Shuinjō tsuketari Shūnōbako Isshiki
name_en: Vermilion-seal certificates held by Hie Shrine, with a full set of storage boxes
description_en: A set of documents with accompanying storage boxes, classified as a tangible cultural property (old documents). They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1991. They are located at Hie Shrine, Nagatachō 2-10-5, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Yorozu Hikaechō
name_en: General memorandum book
description_en: A memorandum book, classified as a tangible cultural property (old documents). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1993. It is located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Imagawa Kōji Kyōdō Kenchiku Kankei Monjo
name_en: Documents relating to the Imagawa Kōji cooperative building
description_en: A collection of documents, classified as a tangible cultural property (old documents). They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2020. They are located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Dōsei Tōrō
name_en: Bronze lanterns
description_en: Bronze lanterns, classified as a tangible cultural property (craft). They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2001. They are located in the precincts of Hie Shrine at Nagatachō 2-10-5, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Dōsei Bonshō (Ōtemachi Nichōme Gobanchi Saki Shutsudo)
name_en: Bronze temple bell (excavated near Ōtemachi 2-chōme 5-banchi)
description_en: A bronze temple bell, classified as a tangible cultural property (craft). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1997. It is located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Dōsei Bonshō (Shinpōji)
name_en: Bronze temple bell (Shinpō-ji Temple)
description_en: A bronze temple bell, classified as a tangible cultural property (craft). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1998. It is located in the precincts of Shinpō-ji Temple at Kōjimachi 6-4-2, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Bunka Ninen Hōnō Sannō Daigongen Shingō Hengaku
name_en: Framed plaque with the divine name Sannō Daigongen, dedicated in Bunka 2
description_en: A framed plaque, classified as a tangible cultural property (craft). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2003. It is located at Hie Shrine, Nagatachō 2-10-5, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Sannō Daigongen Shingō Gaku Hōreki Rokunen Mei
name_en: Plaque with the divine name Sannō Daigongen, inscribed Hōreki 6
description_en: An inscribed plaque, classified as a tangible cultural property (craft). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2011. It is located at Hie Shrine, Nagatachō 2-10-5, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Mokuzō Amida Nyorai Zazō
name_en: Seated wooden statue of Amida Nyorai
description_en: A wooden statue, classified as a tangible cultural property (sculpture). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1999. It is located at Shinpō-ji Temple, Kōjimachi 6-4-2, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Kyū Kodaira Ogawa Yūbinkyokusha
name_en: Former Kodaira Ogawa post office building
description_en: A building, classified as a tangible cultural property (architecture). It is municipally designated by Kodaira City. It is located at Kodaira Furusato Village, Tenjinchō 3-9-1, Kodaira City.

name_romaji: Kaiganji Sanmon
name_en: Main gate of Kaigan-ji Temple
description_en: A temple gate, classified as a tangible cultural property (architecture). It is municipally designated by Kodaira City. It is located in the precincts of Kaigan-ji Temple at Miyukichō 318, Kodaira City.

name_romaji: Kyū Kamiyamake Jūtaku Omoya
name_en: Main building of the former Kamiyama family residence
description_en: A residential building, classified as a tangible cultural property (architecture). It is municipally designated by Kodaira City. It is located at Kodaira Furusato Village, Tenjinchō 3-9-1, Kodaira City.

name_romaji: Kyū Suzukike Jūtaku Kokubitsu
name_en: Grain chest of the former Suzuki family residence
description_en: A grain chest structure, classified as a tangible cultural property (architecture). It is municipally designated by Kodaira City. It is located at Kodaira Furusato Village, Tenjinchō 3-9-1, Kodaira City.

name_romaji: Kyū Ogawake Jūtaku Genkantō
name_en: Entrance building of the former Ogawa family residence
description_en: A residential building, classified as a tangible cultural property (architecture). It is municipally designated by Kodaira City. It is located at Kodaira Furusato Village, Tenjinchō 3-9-1, Kodaira City.

name_romaji: Suzuki Inari Jinja Honden Ōiya no Kote-e
name_en: Plaster relief (kote-e) on the covering structure of the main sanctuary of Suzuki Inari Shrine
description_en: A plaster relief decoration, classified as a tangible cultural property (architecture). It is municipally designated by Kodaira City. It is located in the precincts of the Inari Shrine at Suzukichō 1-510, Kodaira City.

name_romaji: Suzuki Inari Jinja Keidai no Kotohirasha no Chōkoku Sōshoku
name_en: Carved decorations of the Kotohira Shrine in the precincts of Suzuki Inari Shrine
description_en: Carved architectural decorations, classified as a tangible cultural property (architecture). They are municipally designated by Kodaira City. They are located in the precincts of the Inari Shrine at Suzukichō 1-510, Kodaira City.

name_romaji: Koganei Ōju Hi
name_en: Monument of the Koganei cherry trees
description_en: A stone monument, classified as a tangible cultural property (historical material). It is municipally designated by Kodaira City. It is located in the precincts of Kaigan-ji Temple at Miyukichō 318, Kodaira City.

name_romaji: Miyuki Matsu to Miyuki Matsu no Hi
name_en: The Miyuki Pine and the monument of the Miyuki Pine
description_en: A pine tree and its associated monument, classified as a tangible cultural property (historical material). They are municipally designated by Kodaira City. They are located along the Tamagawa Aqueduct in Miyukichō, Kodaira City.
